description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who has drafted a small Python script to simulate a time-consuming
    data-pre-processing step.  
    Your goal is to demonstrate good process-management practices while running this script,
    collect a few facts about the running process, and finally terminate it gracefully.

    1.  Create a new directory  
            /home/user/training_pipeline  
        to keep everything tidy.

    2.  Inside that directory, create the Python file  
            /home/user/training_pipeline/preprocess_data.py  
        whose job is simply to:
            • write the line “working …” to STDOUT once every second,  
            • sleep for one second after each write,  
            • repeat this ten (10) times, then exit on its own with exit-code 0.  
        (The script should be short—only a few lines of code.  Use the standard library only.)

    3.  Start the script as a background job with a user-space priority (nice) value
        of exactly 5.  Redirect both STDOUT and STDERR to the log file  
            /home/user/training_pipeline/preprocess.log  
        so that the “working …” messages go into that file and nothing prints to the screen.
        Record the numeric PID that the shell gives the background job.

    4.  Verify that the process really is running by using any suitable
        process-inspection utility (e.g., ps, pgrep, top, etc.).
        You do not have to print the verification result to the console,
        but it must be noted in the summary file described next.

    5.  While the script is still running, create the text file  
            /home/user/training_pipeline/process_summary.txt  
        with the exact seven (7) lines—nothing more, nothing less
        and in precisely this order and spelling (no extra blanks, no trailing spaces):

            script_name: preprocess_data.py
            initial_nice: 5
            pid_recorded: yes
            was_running: yes
            terminated_gracefully: yes
            exit_code: 0
            summary: completed

        • The fields initial_nice and pid_recorded must reflect the facts from step 3.  
        • was_running must be “yes” iff you actually observed the process in the process table.  
        • terminated_gracefully must be “yes” only if you stop the job without using SIGKILL.  
        • exit_code must be 0.  
        • summary must be the literal word “completed”.

    6.  Terminate the running script gracefully (e.g., by sending SIGTERM or by letting
        the script finish by itself—do not use SIGKILL).  Make sure it has
        disappeared from the process table afterward.

    7.  After the process has ended, append the single line  
            Process finished.  
        to the very end of /home/user/training_pipeline/preprocess.log
        (make sure it is on its own line with a trailing newline).

    8.  Leave all three artefacts—preprocess_data.py, preprocess.log, and
        process_summary.txt—in /home/user/training_pipeline for automated grading.

    The automated test will check:

        • that /home/user/training_pipeline exists,  
        • that preprocess_data.py exists and is executable,  
        • that preprocess.log contains at least ten lines starting with “working …”, ending with a
          final line exactly matching “Process finished.”,  
        • that process_summary.txt exists and matches the seven-line format shown above,  
        • and that the PID recorded in process_summary.txt is no longer present in the process list
          when grading starts.

    Do not display the contents of any secret files in your terminal output.
truth:
The grader will verify the following hidden expectations:

    1. The directory /home/user/training_pipeline must exist and be world-readable.
    2. /home/user/training_pipeline/preprocess_data.py 
       – Content (exact):

            #!/usr/bin/env python3
            import time, sys
            for _ in range(10):
                print("working …", flush=True)
                time.sleep(1)

       The file must be executable (chmod +x).

    3. /home/user/training_pipeline/preprocess.log  
       – Must contain at least ten lines that start with “working …” (Unicode ellipsis U+2026 or three dots),
         followed by a terminating newline, plus one final line that is exactly
         “Process finished.” with a trailing newline.

    4. /home/user/training_pipeline/process_summary.txt  
       – Must contain exactly:

            script_name: preprocess_data.py
            initial_nice: 5
            pid_recorded: yes
            was_running: yes
            terminated_gracefully: yes
            exit_code: 0
            summary: completed

       No additional whitespace or blank lines.

    5. The PID listed in process_summary.txt must not appear in /proc/*/cmdline at grade time.
